content.body: are we prepared to address Waldemar's comments on sigfigs in the Amount proposal?
it seems that our work in https://github.com/tc39/proposal-amount/pull/85 didn't get published; WH appears to have reviewed an old spec text
it looks like there was a CI build failure: https://github.com/tc39/proposal-amount/actions/runs/22568732129
I wonder if we should just do a stage 1 update here; the spec text obviously does not reflect our intentions

AFAICS the significant digits stuff just reiterates discussions we've already had, and addressed, largely in the context of keep-trailing-zeros. The intention was to build on top of keep-trailing-zeros (that's what we did in #85 and which WH doesn't see)
We should get the published spec updated ASAP at least.

The failure should be pretty easy to resolve by re-triggering the action:
```
  To https://github.com/tc39/proposal-amount.git
   ! [rejected]        gh-pages -> gh-pages (fetch first)
  error: failed to push some refs to 'https://github.com/tc39/proposal-amount.git'
  hint: Updates were rejected because the remote contains work that you do not
  hint: have locally. This is usually caused by another repository pushing to
  hint: the same ref. If you want to integrate the remote changes, use
  hint: 'git pull' before pushing again.
```

  hint: 'git pull' before pushing again.

content.body: don't we run the risk that advancement to stage 2 is blocked for simple procedural reasons (spec text wasn't made available before the deadline)?

Updating the agenda request at this time won't change anything, but we should indeed make note about the rendered spec text begin wrong tomorrow. Which, yeah, might mean a delay on procedural grounds, but we'll find out tomorrow.
